replies.author: @daltongalecki683
replies.text:
To everyone responding I genuinely don't know how I can be more clear or explain things better than I already have.

This is simply what we call the "Scunthorpe" problem 

Due to how online chats work especially with online games oftentimes a profanity filter is used. 

However due to the nature of what is deemed offensive changes and evolves as rapidly as language, it tends to make things look worse.

For example:

Foreign names like Nassir are automatically filtered due to it reading the name as N ass er.

The filter will then proceed to do what it was designed to do. 

Older filters will simply make it read as N***er

(However I'm sure you can all see how this actually makes what shouldn't be a problem a bigger problem.)

Therefore new filters will censor the full word. 

Now it will read as ******

Over the years people have found lots of ways to get around these systems like using various symbols $&@ and accents like à î ł.  

So in order to prevent a potential PR disaster, the word believed to be profanity and usually one word before (and sometimes the entire chat, but that's pretty rare now but Marvel Rivals has it.) will be censored.

This leads us to the Current problem

Arc Raiders

Arc is ok.

Raider not so much.

Due to modern filters often being so bloated and companies often over correcting. The filter reads

Arc R(aiders) or Arc R(aid)ers

This leads to the entire thing being censored due to people at one point calling people Aiders as a substitute for the F-slur.

While yes it doesn't mean anything in a literal dictionary. 

Thats not the point.

It's oftentimes better to just censor everything than having lobbies devolve into the good ol' days of cod pre/post game voice/text chats.

For those who still don't really understand.
There is no plot to have people not mention Arc Raiders.

For example as of writing this and testing the Marathon chat the current words are censored.

Puzzle 
Scunthorpe 
Crass
Cocktail 


I'm sure there are lots more but I genuinely don't want to test this for more than I already have.

The main thing is. Filters like these always have things like this happen. It's not new and half the time you get filtered for literally nothing.

text:
dont get the hate tbh people act like there has never been a character select, skill tree and faction select menu in games for like years now lol
I also honestly find it funny when streamers had very similar complaints on arc raiders we called them foolish now same guys are saying the same complaints about marathon and their word is treated in much higher regard now. 
I think many (not all) people are just ready to see this game fail due to its developer and the art style isnt for them specifically. Lets not act like similar systems arent in many other games and many loved games have way worse designed menus, on top of that many of the streamers I see complaining about marathon are the same ones that were saying arc raiders needed a competitive mode, marathon seems like a casual pvp focused extraction shooter with pve elements, it doesnt need to be more then that. not every game needs to be super competitive or super in depth, I think games like arc raiders have shown that much. 
not saying everyone needs to enjoy marathon and dont buy it if it doesnt look like its for you, and thats okay you aren't supposed to want to buy every single game that comes out, its just tiring seeing people put so many games on the execution table before they even come out and paint these games like they are the worst made game ever then go play games with very similar systems and game play and act like its great.

replies.author: @fred6907
replies.text:
"dont get the hate tbh people act like there has never been a character select, skill tree and faction select menu in games for like years now lol"

That sentence perfectly describes what's wrong with the game in the first place. It's just another generic and boring extraction shooter, the market is flooded with similar garbage. You could ask AI to generate a new futuristic ex-shooter, and you'd get this slop.

replies.author: @-chip-3651
replies.text: ​​@fred6907 thats not how game systems work and if you look at the extraction shooter genre as a whole or have played in the genre since the  earlier days youd know there are quite a few things unique to marathon and marathon alone. The genre aswell is honestly much smaller when compared to many other game genres, if you have a issue with the art style tbh thats completely subjective, On top of that games like arc raiders do actually have some similar systems such as the character skill tree and I havent noticed anyone calling that game AI generated, even though it shares many many systems that other games have almost identically. if you are gonna make a genuine criticism of the game Im happy to hear it but using bad faith buzz word phrases like "AI generated" isnt just in poor taste it doesnt make sense in the context of my quote or the conversation around these systems and the extraction shooter genre.

replies.author: @fred6907
replies.text: @-chip-3651 Arc was peak extraction shooter in a market filled with average or less extraction shooters. Anything after Arc will struggle, the bar is set too high. This mediocre confusing mess don't stand a chance.

replies.author: @-chip-3651
replies.text: @fred6907 I heard much of the same when arc raiders released, I heard much of the same when hunt and escape from tarkrov also came out aswell. the games fate will be decided after launch, if it fails it fails but Im not gonna decide a game is a failure before it even comes out and my advice is you shouldnt either nor is your point a genuine criticism of the game. just because a game can be confusing doesnt make it a bad game, there are plenty of games that are confusing until you take the time to learn them just as many  things in life tbh. as for the bar being set high I agree with that, the bar is set high however arc raiders isnt  perfect either, I personally love arc, however competition in the space only serves to make arc and every other extraction shooter better. also arc wasnt the only well made game in the space there were ones before it and there will be ones after. Hunt showdown and Escape from tarkrov are prime examples of shooters that were in the market way before arc raiders, there were also many extraction shooter like games in the space too like the division 2. Id really recommend looking into them they are great games and the genre as a whole has a lot to offer. Marathon doesn't need to surpass arc Raiders to be successful it just needs to find a niche and honestly Im sure it will.

replies.author: @cjameshuff
replies.text:
Yeah...a Doom-style reboot or a System Shock-style remake to get people interested in the franchise and a low-cost, low-risk way to test whether there would be interest to support a bigger game. And the Marathon setting actually has plenty of room to support an extraction shooter game mode: set it a few decades earlier, during the war with the Pfhor, doing raids on Pfhor research facilities guarded by mad Tycho clones for alien technology.

But, with the original being a dark and gritty story-driven shooter with a ton of multiplayer game modes and maps, we now instead just get a Day-Glo/MacDonalds-themed extraction shooter with a few maps, clumsily stapled onto the setting of the original with minimal thought to how they contradicted lore in the process...
